models.model_label: Ontologically unpacked Viral Conceptual Model (VCM) in OntoUML

evidence_quotes.quote: Our ontological unpacking provided a detailed analysis of the complex notions that are hidden within VCM and essential for acquiring a deep understanding of the domain.

A conceptual model provides an information structuring function for the application domain. VCM focuses on genomic data structuring for the purposes of characterizing genomic sequences. However, a conceptual model should also be able to provide conceptual clarification and unambiguous communication .
The results demonstrate the effectiveness of this conceptual model and the usefulness of unpacking a complicated model to improve its clarity, beyond data structuring.

goals.justification: The paper explicitly frames the ontological unpacking of VCM as a means of clarifying concepts and acquiring a deep understanding of the (virus genomics) domain, matching the For Understanding signal patterns ('understand', 'clarify', 'conceptualize').

evidence_quotes.quote: To facilitate a further, robust, application of VCM to projects with other viruses and pathogens, there needs to be some way to assess the quality of the model.
The Sequencing Lab attribute of Sequencing Project incorrectly collapses the concepts of the sequencing laboratory and submitting laboratory, even if they are different entities.
VCM includes all the information about the host sample in one single entity, thus generating a denormalized structure that is conceptually incorrect.

goals.justification: The unpacked model is used to assess the quality of VCM and to identify shortcomings, ambiguities, and conceptual errors (collapsed concepts, underspecified aspects, incorrect denormalization). This matches the For Problem-solving signals 'evaluate', 'identify shortcomings', without going on to implement a system from the model.

evidence_quotes.quote: However, a conceptual model should also be able to provide conceptual clarification and unambiguous communication .

uncertain_goals.reason: This is a general statement about what conceptual models should do, not a description of the unpacked VCM actually being used to communicate between distinct stakeholders or groups.

evidence_quotes.quote: The model is being used for designing: an integrated data warehouse and search system [8]; a linked Phenotype Data Dictionary [3]; a knowledge base of variant impacts [1]; and a visualization engine [6].

uncertain_goals.reason: This describes prior uses of the original VCM (cited via references), not the ontologically unpacked OntoUML model that is the unit of analysis in this paper; the unpacking itself is not framed as guiding implementation.
